mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer spending, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If the cross-sectional rank correlation (rank IC) between RSAFS month-over-month growth and 20-day forward SPX returns is less than 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 2%, it signals a tightening monetary policy that increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: If in episodes where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2%, the 60-day forward SPX return is positive more than 50% of the time or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment growth exceeds 200 thousand per month and the unemployment rate remains below 4%, the labor market is overheating, prompting wage inflation and eventual Fed tightening, which depresses equity prices over the following 20 days.
failure_condition: If during periods meeting the trigger condition, the 20-day forward SPX return is negative less than 55% of the time or the average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: In the late-cycle expansion phase, strong industrial production growth above 5% year-over-year signals supply constraints and capacity exhaustion, which leads to margin compression and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If in late-cycle regimes with INDPRO growth >5%, the 60-day forward SPX return is not negative on average (mean return >= 0) or positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts below 1.2 million units annualized signals weakening residential investment and consumer confidence, foreshadowing economic contraction and negative equity returns over the coming quarter.
failure_condition: If when HOUST falls below 1200, the 60-day forward SPX return is negative less than 60% of the time or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent increases in the consumer price index erode real earnings growth and force the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, both of which are negative for equity valuations over the following month.
failure_condition: If the rank IC between CPIAUCSL month-over-month changes and 20-day forward SPX returns is positive ( > 0.02) over any 3-year rolling window, or if the average forward return in rising inflation periods is positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the Fed funds rate is above 1.5% and unemployment is below 4.5%, the economy is approaching full capacity and imminent tightening is anticipated, leading to short-term equity market weakness over the next five trading days as traders price in policy risk.
failure_condition: If during trigger periods, the 5-day forward SPX return is negative less than 55% of the time or the average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth outpaces retail sales growth over a three-month window, it indicates an inventory buildup that will later be unwound, pressuring corporate profits and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If during such imbalances, the 20-day forward SPX return is not negative on average (mean return >= 0) or positive more than 50% of the time.
